behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local categorization logic; it depends on how git reports rename status in an actual repo. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: using fake diff records can hide subprocess/output-format issues and rename-threshold behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about git change categories, not AI/ML decisions (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows one rename and zero paired add/delete for that path.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real binary content flowing through git subprocess output and CLI decoding/parsing. REAL-INFRA: requires real filesystem + git diff behavior for binary files. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing diff text can miss decode errors/crashes seen only with real binary patches. Rejected higher levels: no model output or multi-agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file contributes to reported counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The critical failure is handling real git behavior in an unborn-HEAD repo (subprocess exit codes/messages). REAL-INFRA: this state is a git/OS repo condition, not just pure function logic. MOCK-HIDE: fake command responses can miss real no-commit edge semantics. Rejected higher levels: behavior contains no AI/ML dependency and no agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/empty structured report and non-error exit behavior.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates correctness against real repository history, requiring true git history traversal via subprocess. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, real subprocess/repo state needed, so not Integration. MOCK-HIDE: fixtures can hide discrepancies in rev ranges/status interpretation that appear with real commits. Rejected higher levels: no LLM/model judgment involved.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with known sequence of adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI for the target window, and compare each category count to ground truth from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about parity across real git index vs working-tree states, which is a subprocess/repo-state boundary. REAL-INFRA: requires manipulating actual staging area and reading CLI output in both modes. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repo states may not reproduce git’s real staged/unstaged semantics. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependence; no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, produce the same file changes once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI in both conditions, and assert identical structured stdout.
